items.title: Multi-class image and object classification
items.description: CNNs and vision transformers use a linear head plus softmax to output class probabilities over ImageNet categories, medical diagnoses, or defect types — one mutually exclusive label per image.

items.title: Language modeling and LLM output heads
items.description: Transformers apply softmax over the vocabulary at each token position to produce next-token probabilities; training minimizes cross-entropy on the true token.

items.title: Text and document categorization
items.description: News topic routing, intent detection with single intent, and sentiment with exclusive classes (positive/neutral/negative) use softmax over K logits.

items.title: Speech and audio class labels
items.description: Phoneme, speaker-ID, or command classifiers output softmax distributions over discrete label sets for downstream decoding or argmax decisions.

items.title: Calibration and temperature scaling
items.description: Post-training temperature divides logits before softmax to adjust confidence without retraining weights — common when deployed probabilities must match empirical accuracy.
